title: astragalus lentiginosus var. variabilis
intro: Astragalus lentiginosus var. variabilis, also known as the spotted milkvetch, is a plant species belonging to the legume family that is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing the risk of infections, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Astragalus lentiginosus var. variabilis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine and modern herbalism.
